content.verdict: Amandari is the original Bali Aman and arguably the most soulful luxury property on the island — a 30-suite village sitting on the edge of the Ayung River gorge in Kedewatan, a ten-minute drive from central Ubud. Thatched alang-alang roofs, paras-stone walls and courtyard layouts were designed in 1989 by Peter Muller to mirror a Balinese banjar, and three decades later it still feels more like a sacred compound than a hotel. Come here for the quietest honeymoon in Ubud, where chanting from the village temple drifts over breakfast.
content.best_room: Book a Valley Suite with private pool — the ones numbered in the 30s face directly into the gorge, with a 10-meter jade pool built into a terraced deck overlooking rice fields and the Ayung. The outdoor sunken tub under the frangipani is where most honeymooners end up at sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Welcome Blessing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private airport transfer from Denpasar (75 minutes). On arrival, a Balinese pemangku performs a melukat purification ceremony in your suite. Dinner at The Restaurant with the rice terrace view, open Indonesian rijsttafel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ayung River & Rice Terraces
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning white-water rafting down the Ayung organized in-house, followed by a private trek through the rice paddies with the resort's cultural guide. Afternoon by your villa pool, sunset cocktails at the Amandari bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Temples of Ubud
itinerary_7_nights.description: Chauffeured day with a local priest-guide visiting Gunung Kawi, Tirta Empul holy water temple (private purification), and Goa Gajah. Dinner at Mozaic or Locavale in Ubud town.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Stillness Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Couples' four-handed Balinese massage and flower bath at the Amandari spa. Private yoga session overlooking the gorge. Room-service dinner on your pool deck with gamelan musicians.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ubud Art & Craft Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit the Agung Rai Museum of Art, the Neka Museum, and a silversmith in Celuk. Private painting class with a Batuan master in the afternoon. Dinner at Bridges Ubud over the Campuhan ravine.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mount Batur Sunrise & Volcano Breakfast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn departure with a guide to summit Mount Batur for sunrise (active hike, about 2 hours up). Breakfast at the crater rim, then relax at Toya Bungkah hot springs before returning to the resort for an afternoon swim.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Ceremony & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private temple blessing with the resort's resident priest to bless your marriage. Late breakfast, final swim, and transfer to Denpasar. The staff line the driveway to wave you off — a small but emotional Amandari tradition.

caveats: No direct beach access — you are in the jungle mountains, a 60-90 minute drive from south Bali beaches.
Suites are open-air in places (bathrooms, walkways); insects and gecko cameos are part of the experience.
This is a contemplative resort — no nightclubs, no swim-up bars. If you want Seminyak energy, stay elsewhere.

faqs.a: No, it accepts families, but the layout, pace and price point mean you will rarely see children. Couples make up roughly 80% of guests.

faqs.q: How far is Amandari from Ubud center?
faqs.a: About 10 minutes by car (5 km) to Ubud Palace. The resort runs a complimentary shuttle several times a day.

faqs.q: Do all suites have private pools?
faqs.a: No — there are 12 pool suites; the rest share the signature 30-meter green-stone main pool that cantilevers over the gorge. Honeymooners should always book a pool suite.

faqs.q: Can Amandari host a wedding or vow renewal?
faqs.a: Yes — the resort specializes in intimate Balinese-blessing ceremonies (8-20 guests) with their own pemangku and in the resort's bale agung. Western legal weddings are handled via a separate civil office in Denpasar.

faqs.q: What's the best time of year to visit?
faqs.a: May-September is the dry season and optimal. April and October are shoulder months with fewer crowds and lower rates. November-March is the green season with daily afternoon downpours but the rice terraces are spectacular.

faqs.q: Is the food worth eating on-property or should we go into Ubud?
faqs.a: The Restaurant is genuinely excellent — the Indonesian tasting menu and the Sunday Balinese megibung feast are destinations in themselves. But Ubud has world-class dining, so mix it up.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking enquiry — 7 nights, pool suite, [DATES]

Dear Amandari reservations team,

We are planning our honeymoon and would like to stay with you for 7 nights from [ARRIVAL DATE] to [DEPARTURE DATE]. We'd like to book a Valley Suite with private pool (ideally in the 30s block with the gorge view).

Could you please confirm:
- Availability and all-in rate including taxes and service
- Honeymoon amenities you typically include (welcome blessing, dinner, spa credit)
- Possibility of a private melukat purification ceremony on arrival
- Airport transfer options and cost
- Any minimum-stay or seasonal surcharge we should be aware of

Thank you very much — we've been dreaming of Amandari for years.

Warm regards,
[Your names]
